event_text: Stephen I is crowned king of Hungary, bringing much of present-day Slovakia into the medieval Hungarian kingdom.
The Mongol invasion devastates areas of present-day Slovakia.
Academia Istropolitana is founded in Pressburg, now Bratislava.
After Mohács, Habsburg rule begins over Royal Hungary including much of present-day Slovakia.
Pressburg, now Bratislava, becomes the capital of Royal Hungary.
Ottoman power in the region declines after the failed siege of Vienna.
Anton Bernolák codifies the first standard Slovak literary language.
Ľudovít Štúr codifies the Slovak literary language based on central Slovak dialects.
The Slovak Uprising seeks national rights during the revolutions in the Habsburg lands.
The Memorandum of the Slovak Nation calls for Slovak political and cultural rights.
Matica slovenská is founded to support Slovak culture and education.
Slovak leaders join the creation of Czechoslovakia.
The Slovak Republic is declared under German influence.
The Slovak National Uprising begins against Nazi Germany and the collaborationist regime.
Czechoslovakia is restored after the Second World War.
Communist rule begins in Czechoslovakia after the February coup.
Czechoslovakia becomes a federation of Czech and Slovak republics.
The Velvet Revolution ends communist rule in Czechoslovakia.
Slovakia becomes an independent state after the dissolution of Czechoslovakia.
Slovakia joins NATO and the European Union.
Slovakia adopts the euro.
